tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src.txt"
dst="$tmpdir/dst.txt"
printf 'payload' > "$src"
"$UTIL" "$src" "$dst"
if [[ -f "$dst" ]] && [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "payload" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "destination does not contain renamed file" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: SOURCE DEST form: documented to rename SOURCE to DEST
tests.expected: Destination path exists as the renamed file with the original contents.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/item"
dir="$tmpdir/target"
mkdir "$dir"
printf 'payload' > "$src"
"$UTIL" "$src" "$dir"
if [[ -f "$dir/item" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "source was not moved into existing directory" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: SOURCE DEST ambiguity: existing DEST directory is the documented move-to-DIRECTORY form
tests.expected: The single source is moved inside the existing destination directory.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/item"
dir="$tmpdir/target"
mkdir "$dir"
printf 'payload' > "$src"
set +e
"$UTIL" -T "$src" "$dir"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "-T unexpectedly accepted a directory destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -T/--no-target-directory: documented to treat DEST as a normal file, not as a target directory
tests.expected: Moving a regular file to an existing directory path under -T fails nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
a="$tmpdir/a"
b="$tmpdir/b"
dir="$tmpdir/dir"
mkdir "$dir"
printf 'a' > "$a"
printf 'b' > "$b"
"$UTIL" "$a" "$b" "$dir"
count=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [[ "$count" == "2" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "multi-source move did not place both files in directory" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: SOURCE... DIRECTORY synopsis: multiple sources are moved into the directory
tests.expected: Both source files appear as entries in the target directory.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
a="$tmpdir/a"
b="$tmpdir/b"
notdir="$tmpdir/notdir"
printf 'a' > "$a"
printf 'b' > "$b"
printf 'x' > "$notdir"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$a" "$b" "$notdir"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "multi-source form accepted a non-directory destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: SOURCE... DIRECTORY synopsis: the final operand must be a directory for multiple sources
tests.expected: Using multiple sources with a non-directory final operand exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
a="$tmpdir/a"
b="$tmpdir/b"
dir="$tmpdir/dir"
mkdir "$dir"
printf 'a' > "$a"
printf 'b' > "$b"
"$UTIL" -t "$dir" "$a" "$b"
count=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [[ "$count" == "2" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "-t did not move all sources into target directory" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -t/--target-directory=DIRECTORY: documented to move all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY
tests.expected: Both source files are moved into the directory named by -t.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
notdir="$tmpdir/notdir"
printf 's' > "$src"
printf 'x' > "$notdir"
set +e
"$UTIL" -t "$notdir" "$src"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "-t accepted a non-directory target" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -t/--target-directory=DIRECTORY: argument is documented as a directory
tests.expected: Using -t with a non-directory target exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/file"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'payload' > "$src"
set +e
"$UTIL" "$src/" "$dst"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "file source with trailing slash was accepted without stripping" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: Trailing SOURCE slash without --strip-trailing-slashes: adjacent category to documented stripping option
tests.expected: A regular-file SOURCE operand with an unstripped trailing slash fails nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/file"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'payload' > "$src"
"$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes "$src/" "$dst"
if [[ -f "$dst" ]] && [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "payload" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--strip-trailing-slashes did not rename file source" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes: documented to remove trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument
tests.expected: The trailing slash is stripped from the file SOURCE and the rename succeeds.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
a="$tmpdir/a"
b="$tmpdir/b"
dir="$tmpdir/dir"
mkdir "$dir"
printf 'a' > "$a"
printf 'b' > "$b"
"$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes -t "$dir" "$a/" "$b/"
count=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -type f | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [[ "$count" == "2" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "trailing slashes were not stripped from each source" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes with multiple SOURCE operands: documented to strip each SOURCE argument
tests.expected: Both regular-file sources with trailing slashes are moved into the target directory.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" -f -n "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "final -n did not prevent overwrite" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: Final option among -i, -f, -n takes effect: -f followed by -n means no-clobber
tests.expected: The existing destination file is not overwritten.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" -n -f "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "new" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "final -f did not overwrite destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: Final option among -i, -f, -n takes effect: -n followed by -f means force overwrite
tests.expected: The existing destination file is overwritten by the source.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
printf 'n\n' | "$UTIL" -f -i "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "interactive no response did not preserve destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -i/--interactive prompts before overwrite and final -i overrides earlier -f; stdin response 'n' skips
tests.expected: With -i and a negative stdin response, the existing destination remains unchanged.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
touch -t 200001010000 "$src"
touch -t 200101010000 "$dst"
"$UTIL" --update "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--update default older replaced a newer destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update with absent UPDATE argument: documented default is older, replacing only older destinations
tests.expected: A newer destination is not replaced when --update is specified without an argument.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
touch -t 200001010000 "$src"
touch -t 200101010000 "$dst"
"$UTIL" "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "new" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "default update mode did not replace destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: No --update option: documented default operation is all, replacing all existing destination files
tests.expected: The existing destination is replaced even though it is newer than the source.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" --update=none "$src" "$dst"
if [[ "$(<"$dst")" == "old" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--update=none overwrote destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=none: documented like --no-clobber, skipped files do not induce failure
tests.expected: The existing destination is left unchanged and the command succeeds.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$src" "$dst"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--update=none-fail did not fail on skipped file" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=none-fail: documented to diagnose skipped files and induce a failure
tests.expected: Skipping an existing destination under none-fail exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=invalid "$src" "$dst"
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "invalid --update value was accepted" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=UPDATE documented value set is {all,none,none-fail,older}; outside value should be rejected
tests.expected: An UPDATE value outside the documented set exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" --backup=simple "$src" "$dst"
if [[ -f "$dst~" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "simple backup with default tilde suffix was not created" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup[=CONTROL] with simple control and default backup suffix '~'
tests.expected: Overwriting an existing destination creates a simple backup named with a trailing tilde.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" --backup=numbered "$src" "$dst"
if [[ -f "$dst.~1~" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "numbered backup was not created" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup=numbered: documented to make numbered backups
tests.expected: The first numbered backup is created using the numbered backup naming form.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
src="$tmpdir/src"
dst="$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'new' > "$src"
printf 'old' > "$dst"
"$UTIL" --backup=none "$src" "$dst"
count=$(find "$tmpdir" -maxdepth 1 -name 'dst*~' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
if [[ "$count" == "0" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--backup=none created a backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup control value none/off: documented to never make backups even if --backup is given
tests.expected: No tilde-style backup file is created when backup control is none.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
left="$tmpdir/left"
right="$tmpdir/right"
printf 'left-data' > "$left"
printf 'right-data' > "$right"
"$UTIL" --exchange "$left" "$right"
if [[ "$(<"$left")" == "right-data" ]]; then
  exit 0
else
  echo "--exchange did not swap file contents" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --exchange: documented to exchange source and destination
tests.expected: After exchange, the former destination contents are at the source path.
